behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is about moderation quality of the vision model (detecting explicit content). A stubbed classifier can only confirm routing, not whether real model judgments catch explicit images. No multi-step agent pipeline is required beyond one model decision plus downstream hold logic, so Workflow is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of explicit images through the real moderation path using the real vision model, then assert each post is marked flagged and routed to human-review hold state (not published). Include near-threshold explicit samples to catch real misclassification failures.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly model-output quality under adversarial text transformations. Deterministic mocks would hide failures in real model understanding of obfuscation. It is a single agentic classification behavior, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real text-model moderation on a dataset of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks), and assert they are consistently flagged and held. Include controls that should not be flagged to detect overblocking drift.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: image result + text result + publish path. The behavior does not require validating model judgment quality; it only requires that when both checks return pass, publish occurs and hold/drop does not. That concrete scope disqualifies Agentic/Workflow here.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision engine, and publish/hold persistence together with deterministic pass results from image/text check modules; submit a post and assert final state is published, with no hold record and no drop/loss in storage/queue transitions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level rejected because behavior text is conditional on 'passes both content checks' and tests downstream state transition, not correctness of model outputs.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO across real infrastructure boundaries (network calls, queueing, service runtime). In-process fakes can miss true timeout/latency behavior. Agentic quality is not under test; this is plumbing/performance of the full running system.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end load scenario against the running moderation stack with real HTTP submission path and real downstream services, measuring p95/p99 completion time from submit to final decision; assert completion remains <=5s under peak-like concurrency.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected because behavior text evaluates timing of pipeline completion, not classification quality/accuracy.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is error-path orchestration between moderation service and retry queue when vision dependency fails. This can be reproduced with deterministic dependency-failure injection; no real model output judgment is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation service with a vision-client test double that returns unavailability errors/timeouts; submit image posts and assert they are enqueued for retry with correct status/metadata and are never auto-approved or published immediately.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network/OS failure modes, only correct fallback state transition on unavailability.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: The behavior requires repeated real model-involved decisions across sequential submissions and consistency over time/window state. A single-call agentic test is insufficient because the failure mode is cross-step consistency (drift/instability/cache-window interaction) across multiple agentic executions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly (e.g., N runs within the configured short window) through the real moderation pipeline and assert decision consistency across runs, while recording whether consistency comes from stable model output or intended windowed decision reuse.
